info.name: Airtable Metadata API
info.description:
The Airtable Metadata API provides access to base and schema management operations. You can list bases, retrieve base schemas with table and field definitions, create new bases, tables, and fields, and update table and field configurations programmatically. This API enables developers to introspect and modify the structure of their Airtable data.

Contact Support:
 Name: Airtable
 Email: support@airtable.com

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').
(Required) The unique identifier of the table (starts with 'tbl').
(Required) The unique identifier of the field (starts with 'fld').

request.description: Updates the name, description, or configuration options of an existing field. Only the provided properties will be modified. Not all field properties can be updated after creation.

response.name: The updated field definition.

item.name: Airtable Create a Field in a Table

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').
(Required) The unique identifier of the table (starts with 'tbl').

request.description: Creates a new field in the specified table. The field must include a name and type. Some field types (like singleSelect, multipleSelects) accept additional options for configuration. Note that formula and some computed field types cannot be created via the API.

response.name: The newly created field definition.

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').

request.description: Returns the schema of the specified base, including all tables, their fields (with type and configuration), and views. This endpoint provides a complete structural overview of the base.

response.name: The base schema with tables, fields, and views.

item.name: Airtable Create a Table in a Base

variable.description: (Required) The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').

request.description: Creates a new table within the specified base. The table must include a name and at least one field definition. Optionally include a description for the table.

response.name: The newly created table with its schema.

request.description: Returns a paginated list of all bases that the authenticated user has access to. Each base includes its ID, name, and permission level. Use the offset parameter from the response to paginate through all available bases.

response.name: A paginated list of bases.

item.name: Airtable Get Current User and Scopes

request.description: Returns the ID and scopes of the currently authenticated user or service account. Useful for verifying authentication and checking available permissions.

response.name: The current user ID and authorized scopes.
